phase2.errorMessage:
Test.java:6: error: incompatible types: String cannot be converted to AutoCloseable
        try (String s = "Hello") {  // String doesn't implement AutoCloseable!
            ^
1 error
phase2.explanation:
try-with-resources only works with AutoCloseable resources!

String doesn't implement AutoCloseable, so you can't use it in try-with-resources.

FIX: Only use resources that implement AutoCloseable:
• BufferedReader ✓
• Scanner ✓
• FileReader ✓
• String ✗
• Integer ✗

Always check if a class implements AutoCloseable before using it in try-with-resources.

phase3.prompt:
Write a program that:
1. Uses try-with-resources to create a Scanner reading from a file called "data.txt"
2. Reads and prints all lines from the file
3. Catches IOException if file not found

Note: For this exercise, assume the file exists with some sample text.
dialogueHints: Use File or FileReader to open the file
